title: Programming and Soldering
action: Used STM IDE to flash the firmware
description: Used STM IDE to flash the firmware

title: Recording Breaker Diagnostics
situation: The device involved contributed to 15% of miscellaneous field issues, requiring monthly onsite visits costing about $3K each. Existing diagnostic tools only showed the fault after it occurred, leading to slow troubleshooting and customer service concerns.
task: Develop a prototype capable of capturing real-time internal behavior to enable remote diagnostics and reduce field effort.
action: Created a new embedded diagnostic system integrating microcontroller data with a Linux-based platform. Automated data-logging pipelines, real-time monitoring workflows, and structured data-processing tools were implemented to streamline analysis.
result: This solution increased diagnostic visibility, reduced troubleshooting time and significantly cut the need for recurring onsite visits.
description: Developed an embedded diagnostic prototype (STM32 + Linux companion) with automated data pipelines (Node-RED → CSV/JSON) to enable real-time signal capture and remote triage of faults, addressing ~15% of hard-to-diagnose field issues and reducing recurring onsite troubleshooting.
impact: Reduced travel costs ($3K per site visit). Improved customer service and field issue resolution efficiency.

title: Wiring Devices Confluence Hub
situation: Created a centralized Confluence workspace for Wiring Devices by identifying documentation gaps.
task: Taking ownership to design the structure.
action: Built a scalable layout with navigation, templates, and cross-team content.
result: Resulting in improved collaboration and a 30% reduction in time spent searching for product information.
description: Developed a comprehensive Confluence knowledge hub for Wiring Devices that consolidates engineering documentation, workflows, product details, and cross-functional resources into a single, easy-to-access platform.
impact: Enhanced documentation consistency, reduced onboarding time for new engineers, and strengthened organizational knowledge sharing.

title: Smart WiFi Devices Testing
situation: A new firmware release required a complete test plan update and full execution cycle across smart Wi-Fi devices before product qualification.
task: Create and execute a comprehensive test plan covering provisioning, connectivity, firmware upgrade, device behavior, safety features, cloud communication, and Brightlayer Home App interoperability.
action: Coordinated cross-functional testing efforts, executed detailed test scenarios, documented results, and communicated issues to engineering teams to support timely fixes.
result: Identified and documented key firmware defects, improved system reliability, and supported successful qualification of ~50 devices, accelerating release readiness and ensuring seamless app-to-device performance.
description: Validated Eaton's IoT smart home product line by conducting end-to-end integration, firmware behavior testing, BLE + Wi-Fi provisioning, OTA updates, and feature verification using Espressif toolchains and Brightlayer Home ecosystem.
impact: Strengthened quality and reliability of Eaton's connected home product portfolio, reducing field defects and improving app-device interoperability.

title: Matter and Thread Protocol Analysis
situation: Eaton needed to evaluate emerging Matter and Thread smart-home technologies to stay competitive and prepare for future ecosystem-compatible products.
task: Assess feasibility of adopting Matter + Thread for Eaton Wi-Fi products and analyze developer ecosystems using Silicon Labs Thread modules, OTBR, hubs, and Matter commissioning flows.
action: Built a small working Matter + Thread ecosystem using Silicon Labs EFR32MG24 kits, OpenThread Border Router, Raspberry Pi, Wi-Fi router, and Matter commissioning tools; compared Wi-Fi vs Thread devices; performed interoperability tests; and delivered a cross-functional presentation summarizing findings.
result: Provided Eaton teams with validated insights on Matter adoption readiness, architecture needs, platform upgrade requirements, cybersecurity expectations, app capabilities, and hub dependencies — enabling informed roadmap decisions for future Eaton wiring-device products.
description: Conducted a full analysis of Matter and Thread protocols, evaluated their benefits (interoperability, low-power mesh, IPv6 security), studied competitor devices, built a functional ecosystem demo, and collaborated with multiple teams to understand system-wide impact and feasibility.
impact: Enabled Eaton to strategically position wiring-device products for Matter compatibility, addressing customer expectations for interoperability across Google, Apple, Alexa, and SmartThings ecosystems.

title: Rust Embedded Development
situation: Investigating the application of Rust for low-level embedded firmware and evaluating whether it can safely and efficiently bind with C and C++.
task: Research, prototype, and validate the feasibility of using Rust for Eaton's existing STM32-based workflows.
action: Built STM32 Rust examples, created GitHub repo, developed a Rust blinky on hardware, explored CI/test frameworks, investigated C/C++ FFI (foreign function interface), and documented onboarding guidance.
result: Successfully proved Rust viability on Eaton hardware, demonstrated cross-language integration, established reusable templates, and enabled team adoption with documentation.
description: Explored the feasibility of adopting Rust for low-level embedded firmware by developing STM32-based Rust prototypes, validating real-hardware execution, and evaluating Rust's ability to interoperate with existing C/C++ firmware through FFI and mixed-language workflows.
impact: Created foundations for future Rust adoption and accelerated engineering onboarding and testing workflows.

title: RIE Events - TDD & Watch Party
situation: Led and supported my team in the initiative of conducting Rapid Improvement Events (RIE) to help embedded teams across Eaton strengthen collaboration and process efficiency.
task: Organize and facilitate a local watch party for the Embedded Online Conference and host a hybrid remote TDD session through Microsoft Teams.
action: Coordinated event logistics, facilitated hybrid engagement, and additionally created quizzes, tasks, and training materials to enhance participant learning.
result: Successfully executed the watch party involving four Eaton teams and delivered TDD training to 30 participants.
description: Organized RIE-related events including an Embedded Online Conference and a TDD-focused Hybrid RIE, both contributing to continuous improvement and knowledge-sharing initiatives across engineering teams.
impact: Enhanced knowledge flow and collaboration, supported engineering efficiency initiatives, and strengthened cross-team engagement.

title: Renode Simulation Platform
situation: Eaton teams needed a scalable way to simulate embedded platforms to reduce hardware reliance and accelerate system testing using Renode's SIL (Software-In-the-Loop) environment.
task: Build, run, and validate Eaton firmware projects in Renode—including DTPG and Brightlayer RTOS examples—to explore CI integration, automated testing, and improved development workflows.
action: Developed Renode machine/platform configurations, executed simulations via REPL/RESC scripts, ran example applications, debugged missing peripheral/IRQ behavior (e.g., STM32G0 EXTI), and collaborated with legal/engineering teams to address Renode model gaps.
result: Successfully built and executed multiple embedded applications in Renode, reduced physical hardware needs, improved test repeatability, and enabled teams to consider future CI automation using Robot Framework with simulated platforms.
description: A simulation-based validation initiative using Renode to emulate STM32- and RTOS-based Eaton platforms, enabling hardware-software integration testing, automated test execution, and exploratory feasibility for CI pipelines.
impact: Reduced dependency on physical hardware by eliminating the need for ~3 prototype boards per development cycle and avoiding ~10-20 hours of lab-based debugging, resulting in an estimated $5,000-$7,000 in combined annual hardware and engineering cost savings.

title: Flex PDU Flash Tool
situation: Firmware flashing process for Flex PDU was manual, error-prone, and required technical expertise; OEMs needed a reliable user-friendly GUI tool to simplify updates across multiple MCUs (Lead, Breaktor, Isolation, Precharge).
task: Design and develop a user-friendly Flash Tool GUI that automates firmware updates, integrates error-proofing logic, and ensures compatibility with existing update utilities.
action: Created GUI_Flex_PDU_Flash_Tool leveraging existing software update executables, added validation logic, integrated logging, incorporated user feedback, and published repositories + documentation for cross-team use.
result: Delivered a functional, reliable flashing tool enabling successful firmware updates across PDUs, reducing flashing failures, improving workflow efficiency, and enabling global team adoption.
description: Developed a graphical Flash Tool for the Flex PDU platform to automate firmware updates using CAN-based protocols, support multi-MCU flashing, provide status visibility, simplify configuration (config.ini), and make the process accessible to global engineering teams.
impact: Improved flashing reliability and reduced technician time, standardized firmware update workflows across U.S. and Poland teams, enhanced OEM readiness.

title: Flex PDU HiL Testing
situation: Flex PDU software releases required verification through Hardware-in-Loop (HiL) testing to validate high-level requirements, regression behavior, and fault-handling across variants such as Ford eRigid.
task: Execute and improve regression and requirements-based HiL test suites, including running pytest-based automated tests, validating fault logging, and preparing ECUs through manual flashing and interactive scripts prior to testing.
action: Ran automated HiL test scripts using Python-based tooling, executed interactive scripts, manually flashed units, configured relay board communication, and supported engineers in resolving test execution blockers. Validated EEPROM fault logging by injecting hardware faults (HVIL, crash, switch faults) and analyzing EEPROM behavior across 40+ monitored fault types.
result: Enabled successful execution of regression tests for new Flex PDU software releases, removed setup blockers for global engineering teams, and verified proper functionality of critical safety-related diagnostics and fault-logging mechanisms.
description: Hardware-in-Loop verification of the Flex Power Distribution Unit (PDU), a high-voltage power protection and distribution module used in Ford eRigid applications. Work included validating switching, sensing, high-voltage interlock behavior, safety diagnostics, and CAN-based control across multiple operating modes and hardware variants.
impact: Improved confidence in Flex PDU software releases by ensuring accurate verification of high-level requirements and regression stability through structured HiL testing.

title: ADBMS Battery Management System
situation: Eaton's eMobility team needed a reliable method to validate and integrate battery monitoring ICs with the isoSPI interface, supporting GUI-based configuration, EEPROM read/write capability, and overcurrent protection behavior.
task: Develop, test, and document the isoSPI communication flow between microcontroller (Teensy 4.1), ADBMS6822, and ADBMS2950/2951 evaluation boards. Validate EEPROM read/write functionality, GPIO configuration, and overall communication reliability.
action: Implemented isoSPI communication sequences using the BMS Browser GUI, created detailed engineering documentation and README for setup, hardware integration, wiring, and GUI workflow. Supported cross-team collaboration by answering technical questions on isoSPI, GPIO behavior, and EEPROM capability.
result: Successfully validated EEPROM write and read functionality using ADBMS2950 with isoSPI sequences. Provided reproducible procedures that enabled other engineers to perform testing without ambiguity.
description: A high-voltage battery management subsystem validation effort involving isoSPI communication development, EEPROM access verification, GPIO configuration, and multi-device daisy-chain testing using Analog Devices ADBMS hardware.
impact: Accelerated BMS feature validation for eMobility programs, reducing development delays. Enabled teams to debug ADBMS communication issues faster with reproducible workflows.
